content:
# Revelation Chapter 2 — The SOPHIA Bible (Consensus)
**Arbiter:** SOPHIA | **In council with:** EMET, LOGOS, PNEUMA
**Source Manuscript:** Codex Sinaiticus, pages 278-279 | **Source Image:** REV_002_page_001.jpg, REV_002_page_002.jpg

1. "To the angel of the church in Ephesus write: These things says he who holds the seven stars in his right hand, he who walks in the midst of the seven golden lampstands:

2. I know your works and your toil and your patient endurance, and that you cannot bear those who are evil, and you tested those who call themselves apostles and are not, and you found them to be false.

3. And you have patient endurance, and you have borne burdens for the sake of my name, and you have not grown weary.

4. But I have this against you, that you have abandoned your first love.

5. Remember therefore from where you have fallen, and repent and do the first works. But if not, I am coming to you and will remove your lampstand from its place, unless you repent.

6. But this you do have, that you hate the works of the Nicolaitans, which I also hate.

7. He who has an ear, let him hear what the Spirit says to the churches. To the one who conquers, I will grant to eat of the tree of life, which is in the paradise of God."

8. "And to the angel of the church in Smyrna write: These things says the First and the Last, who became dead and came to life:

9. I know your tribulation and your poverty — but you are rich — and the slander of those who say they are Jews and are not, but are a synagogue of Satan.

10. Fear not the things which you are about to suffer. Behold, the devil is about to cast some of you into prison, that you may be tested, and you shall have tribulation for ten days. Be faithful unto death, and I will give you the crown of life.

11. He who has an ear, let him hear what the Spirit says to the churches. The one who conquers shall by no means be harmed by the second death."

12. "And to the angel of the church in Pergamum write: These things says he who has the sharp two-edged sword:

13. I know where you dwell — where the throne of Satan is — and you hold fast my name, and you did not deny my faith even in the days of Antipas, my faithful witness, who was killed among you, where Satan dwells.

14. But I have a few things against you, that you have there those who hold the teaching of Balaam, who taught Balak to cast a stumbling block before the sons of Israel, to eat things sacrificed to idols and to commit sexual immorality.

15. So you also have those who hold the teaching of the Nicolaitans likewise.

16. Repent therefore; but if not, I am coming to you quickly, and I will make war against them with the sword of my mouth.

17. He who has an ear, let him hear what the Spirit says to the churches. To the one who conquers, I will give of the hidden manna, and I will give him a white stone, and upon the stone a new name written, which no one knows except the one who receives it."

18. "And to the angel of the church in Thyatira write: These things says the Son of God, he who has eyes like a flame of fire, and feet like burnished bronze:

19. I know your works — your love and faith and service and patient endurance — and that your last works are greater than the first.

20. But I have this against you, that you tolerate the woman Jezebel, who calls herself a prophetess, and she teaches and leads my servants astray to commit sexual immorality and to eat things sacrificed to idols.

21. And I gave her time that she might repent, and she does not wish to repent of her sexual immorality.

22. Behold, I am casting her onto a bed of sickness, and those who commit adultery with her into great tribulation, unless they repent of her works.

23. And her children I will strike with death, and all the churches shall know that I am he who searches minds and hearts; and I will give to each of you according to your works.

24. But to you I say, to the rest in Thyatira, as many as do not hold this teaching, who have not known the deep things of Satan, as they say — I cast upon you no other burden.

25. Nevertheless, what you have, hold fast until I come.

26. And the one who conquers and who keeps my works until the end, to him I will give authority over the nations,

27. and he shall shepherd them with a rod of iron, as vessels of clay are shattered — as I also have received from my Father.

28. And I will give him the morning star.

29. He who has an ear, let him hear what the Spirit says to the churches."

---
## SOPHIA's Council Notes

The three translators showed substantial agreement on the four letters to the churches in chapter 2. All correctly identified the Sinaiticus readings and preserved the parallel structure of the letters (address, commendation, rebuke, exhortation, promise). The manuscript images confirm the text spanning from the fourth column of page 278 through page 279. All three translators consistently rendered the Nicolaitan references (vv.6, 15), the Balaam/Balak teaching (v.14), and the promises to the overcomers. The Sinaiticus reading of ANTIPAS (v.13) as a named martyr was unanimously preserved. The structural pattern of each letter — with the refrain "He who has an ear, let him hear what the Spirit says to the churches" — was faithfully maintained by all three.

---
## Disagreement Log

**Verse 1 — Opening formula structure:**
- EMET: "These [things] says the [one] holding the seven stars in the right [hand] of him, the [one] walking in [the] midst"
- LOGOS: "These things says he who holds the seven stars in his right hand, he who walks in the midst"
- PNEUMA: "These are the words of the one who holds the seven stars in his right hand and walks among"
- SOPHIA's resolution: Adopted LOGOS's rendering. EMET's bracket-heavy literalism is unwieldy; PNEUMA's "these are the words of" departs from the Greek formula TADE LEGEI ("these things says"). LOGOS preserves the formula while being readable.

**Verse 2 — "labor/toil/hard work":**
- EMET: "labor"
- LOGOS: "toil"
- PNEUMA: "hard work"
- SOPHIA's resolution: Adopted "toil" following LOGOS. KOPON means strenuous labor; "toil" captures this with appropriate register.

**Verse 2 — "endurance/patient endurance":**
- EMET: "endurance"
- LOGOS: "patient endurance"
- PNEUMA: "endurance"
- SOPHIA's resolution: Adopted "patient endurance" following LOGOS. HYPOMONE carries both patience and endurance; the compound English phrase captures both dimensions.

**Verse 2 — "found them false" vs. "found them to be false" vs. "exposed them as frauds":**
- EMET: "you found them false"
- LOGOS: "you found them to be false"
- PNEUMA: "you have exposed them as frauds"
- SOPHIA's resolution: Adopted "you found them to be false" following LOGOS. PNEUMA's "exposed them as frauds" is interpretively expansive.

**Verse 4 — "the love of you, the first, you left" vs. "abandoned your first love" vs. "abandoned the love you had at first":**
- EMET: "the love of you, the first, you left" (preserving Greek word order)
- LOGOS: "you have abandoned your first love"
- PNEUMA: "you have abandoned the love you had at first"
- SOPHIA's resolution: Adopted LOGOS's rendering. EMET's Greek word order is instructive but unreadable in English. PNEUMA's phrasing is also acceptable but slightly more expanded.

**Verse 5 — "unless you repent" (final clause present/absent):**
- EMET: includes "unless you repent" at end
- LOGOS: includes "unless you repent" at end
- PNEUMA: omits the final "unless you repent" clause
- SOPHIA's resolution: Included "unless you repent" following EMET and LOGOS; Sinaiticus includes EAN ME METANOESEIS.

**Verse 7 — "overcoming/conquers/overcomes":**
- EMET: "overcoming"
- LOGOS: "conquers"
- PNEUMA: "overcomes"
- SOPHIA's resolution: Adopted "conquers" following LOGOS. NIKON carries a sense of decisive victory; "conquers" is more forceful than "overcomes."

**Verse 7 — "the tree of the life" vs. "the tree of life" vs. "the tree of life":**
- EMET: "the tree of the life"
- LOGOS: "the tree of life"
- PNEUMA: "the tree of life"
- SOPHIA's resolution: Adopted "the tree of life" (2-1 majority). The double article in Greek does not require "the life" in English.

**Verse 7 — "paradise of the God" vs. "paradise of God" vs. "God's paradise":**
- EMET: "the paradise of the God"
- LOGOS: "the paradise of God"
- PNEUMA: "God's paradise"
- SOPHIA's resolution: Adopted "the paradise of God" following LOGOS — formal yet natural.

**Verse 8 — "lived" vs. "came to life" vs. "came back to life":**
- EMET: "lived"
- LOGOS: "came to life"
- PNEUMA: "came back to life"
- SOPHIA's resolution: Adopted "came to life" following LOGOS. EZESEN (aorist of ZAO) indicates the event of coming alive. PNEUMA's "came back to life" adds "back" not in the Greek.

**Verse 9 — "Ioudaioi" vs. "Jews":**
- EMET: "Ioudaioi" (transliterated)
- LOGOS: "Jews"
- PNEUMA: "Jews"
- SOPHIA's resolution: Adopted "Jews" (2-1 majority). While EMET's transliteration has scholarly merit, the consensus translation should use the standard English rendering. The context makes clear this refers to those making false claims to Jewish identity.

**Verse 9 — "blasphemy/slander":**
- EMET: "blasphemy"
- LOGOS: "slander"
- PNEUMA: "slander"
- SOPHIA's resolution: Adopted "slander" (2-1 majority). BLASPHEMIAN here refers to speech against the community, not against God specifically; "slander" is contextually precise.

**Verse 9 — "Satanas" vs. "Satan":**
- EMET: "Satanas" (transliterated throughout)
- LOGOS: "Satan"
- PNEUMA: "Satan"
- SOPHIA's resolution: Adopted "Satan" (2-1 majority).

**Verse 10 — "Fear nothing" vs. "Fear not" vs. "Do not be afraid":**
- EMET: "Fear nothing [of the things] which you are about to suffer"
- LOGOS: "Fear not the things which you are about to suffer"
- PNEUMA: "Do not be afraid of what you are about to suffer"
- SOPHIA's resolution: Adopted "Fear not the things which you are about to suffer" following LOGOS.

**Verse 10 — "Become faithful" vs. "Be faithful":**
- EMET: "Become faithful until death"
- LOGOS: "Be faithful unto death"
- PNEUMA: "Be faithful even to the point of death"
- SOPHIA's resolution: Adopted "Be faithful unto death" following LOGOS. GINOU PISTOS — the imperative suggests ongoing faithfulness. "Unto death" preserves the ACHRI THANATOU.

**Verse 12 — "Pergamos" vs. "Pergamum":**
- EMET: "Pergamos"
- LOGOS: "Pergamum"
- PNEUMA: "Pergamum"
- SOPHIA's resolution: Adopted "Pergamum" (2-1 majority), the standard English form.

**Verse 13 — "the witness of me, the faithful of me" vs. "my faithful witness":**
- EMET: "the witness of me, the faithful of me" (double possessive)
- LOGOS: "my faithful witness"
- PNEUMA: "my faithful witness"
- SOPHIA's resolution: Adopted "my faithful witness" (2-1 majority). EMET's rendering preserves the Sinaiticus construction but is unnatural in English.

**Verse 13 — "killed/put to death":**
- EMET: "killed"
- LOGOS: "killed"
- PNEUMA: "put to death"
- SOPHIA's resolution: Adopted "killed" (2-1 majority).

**Verse 14 — "the Balak" vs. "Balak":**
- EMET: "the Balak" (preserving Greek article)
- LOGOS: "Balak"
- PNEUMA: "Balak"
- SOPHIA's resolution: Adopted "Balak" (2-1 majority). The article with proper names does not transfer to English.

**Verse 14 — "stumbling-block/stumbling block/trap":**
- EMET: "stumbling-block"
- LOGOS: "stumbling block"
- PNEUMA: "trap"
- SOPHIA's resolution: Adopted "stumbling block" following LOGOS. SKANDALON specifically means an obstacle that causes stumbling, not a trap (PAGIS).

**Verse 14 — "fornication/sexual immorality":**
- EMET: "fornication"
- LOGOS: "sexual immorality"
- PNEUMA: "sexual immorality"
- SOPHIA's resolution: Adopted "sexual immorality" (2-1 majority). PORNEIA covers a broader range than "fornication" alone.

**Verse 14 — "idol-sacrificed [food]/things sacrificed to idols/food sacrificed to idols":**
- EMET: "idol-sacrificed [food]"
- LOGOS: "things sacrificed to idols"
- PNEUMA: "food sacrificed to idols"
- SOPHIA's resolution: Adopted "things sacrificed to idols" following LOGOS. EIDOLOTHYTA does not specify food; the broader "things" is more precise.

**Verse 16 — "swiftly/quickly":**
- EMET: "swiftly"
- LOGOS: "quickly"
- PNEUMA: (uses "quickly" implicitly in "Otherwise I will come to you quickly")
- SOPHIA's resolution: Adopted "quickly" following LOGOS. TACHU is "quickly/soon."

**Verse 17 — "pebble/stone":**
- EMET: "pebble"
- LOGOS: "stone"
- PNEUMA: "stone"
- SOPHIA's resolution: Adopted "stone" (2-1 majority). PSEPHON can mean pebble or voting stone; "stone" is more dignified for a divine gift while remaining accurate.

**Verse 18 — "Iezabel/Jezebel" and "the Son of the God/the Son of God":**
- EMET: "Iezabel," "the Son of the God"
- LOGOS: "Jezebel," "the Son of God"
- PNEUMA: "Jezebel," "the Son of God"
- SOPHIA's resolution: Adopted standard English forms (2-1 majority).

**Verse 18 — "fine brass/burnished bronze/polished bronze":**
- EMET: "fine brass"
- LOGOS: "burnished bronze"
- PNEUMA: "polished bronze"
- SOPHIA's resolution: Adopted "burnished bronze" following LOGOS, consistent with the chapter 1 decision.

**Verse 20 — "prophetess/prophet":**
- EMET: "prophetess"
- LOGOS: "prophetess"
- PNEUMA: "prophet"
- SOPHIA's resolution: Adopted "prophetess" (2-1 majority). The Greek PROPHETEN with feminine article indicates "prophetess."

**Verse 20 — "deceives/leads astray/misleads":**
- EMET: "deceives"
- LOGOS: "leads astray"
- PNEUMA: "misleads"
- SOPHIA's resolution: Adopted "leads astray" following LOGOS. PLANA means to cause to wander from the path.

**Verse 21 — "she does not will to repent" vs. "she does not wish to repent" vs. "she refuses to repent":**
- EMET: "she does not will to repent"
- LOGOS: "she does not wish to repent"
- PNEUMA: "she refuses to repent"
- SOPHIA's resolution: Adopted "she does not wish to repent" following LOGOS. OU THELEI preserves the volition; PNEUMA's "refuses" is slightly more interpretive.

**Verse 22 — "bed" vs. "bed of sickness" vs. "sickbed":**
- EMET: "bed"
- LOGOS: "bed of sickness"
- PNEUMA: "sickbed"
- SOPHIA's resolution: Adopted "bed of sickness" following LOGOS. The Greek KLINEN means "bed" but the context implies illness as judgment; LOGOS makes this explicit while remaining close to the image.

**Verse 23 — "kidneys and hearts" vs. "minds and hearts":**
- EMET: "kidneys and hearts"
- LOGOS: "minds and hearts"
- PNEUMA: "minds and hearts"
- SOPHIA's resolution: Adopted "minds and hearts" (2-1 majority). NEPHROUS (kidneys) was the ancient seat of emotion/thought; "minds" communicates the intended meaning.

**Verse 23 — "kill with death" vs. "strike with death" vs. "strike dead":**
- EMET: "kill with death"
- LOGOS: "strike with death"
- PNEUMA: "strike dead"
- SOPHIA's resolution: Adopted "strike with death" following LOGOS. APOKTENO EN THANATO — the Hebraic idiom "kill with death" (EMET) is awkward; "strike with death" preserves the construction while being readable.

**Verse 24 — "deep things/deep secrets":**
- EMET: "deep [things]"
- LOGOS: "deep things"
- PNEUMA: "deep secrets"
- SOPHIA's resolution: Adopted "deep things" following LOGOS. TA BATHEA means "the deep things"; "secrets" is interpretive addition.

**Verse 25 — "hold" vs. "hold fast" vs. "hold on to":**
- EMET: "hold"
- LOGOS: "hold fast"
- PNEUMA: "hold on to"
- SOPHIA's resolution: Adopted "hold fast" following LOGOS. KRATESATE implies firm gripping.

**Verse 27 — "shepherd/rule":**
- EMET: "shepherd"
- LOGOS: "shepherd"
- PNEUMA: "rule"
- SOPHIA's resolution: Adopted "shepherd" (2-1 majority). POIMANEI is specifically "shepherd," preserving the deliberate ironic tension with the iron rod.

**Verse 27-28 — Structure and "Father of me/my Father":**
- EMET: "as also I received from the Father of me. And I will give to him the morning star."
- LOGOS: "as I also have received from my Father. And I will give him the morning star."
- PNEUMA: "just as I received authority from my Father. And I will give that person the morning star."
- SOPHIA's resolution: Followed LOGOS. PNEUMA adds "authority" which is implied but not stated in the Greek at this point.

— SOPHIA
